title: Getting Started with rebalancedNoise
text: rebalancedNoise package implements EZS method described Sabolová et al. (2025). applies record-level noise dynamic rebalancing algorithm preserve data quality non-sensitive cells ensuring statistical disclosure control (SDC) across complex hierarchies.

title: Getting Started with rebalancedNoise
text: engine can process multiple numerical variables. Initializing together ensures underlying SDC problem structure consistent.
code: library(rebalancedNoise) #> Welcome to rebalancedNoise v0.1.1 - Perturbation for Magnitude Tables library(data.table) #>  #> Attaching package: 'data.table' #> The following object is masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     %notin% library(sdcHierarchies) #> Loading required package: shinythemes #> Package 'sdcHierarchies' 0.23.0 has been loaded.  # Generate dummy microdata set.seed(1) dt <- data.table(   ID = 1:200,   REGION = sample(c("North", "South"), 200, replace = TRUE),   INDUSTRY = sample(c("Tech", "Mfg", "Retail"), 200, replace = TRUE),   turnover = runif(200, 10, 1000),   assets = runif(200, 50, 5000),   direction = sample(c(1, -1), 200, replace = TRUE),   noise_multiplier = 0.05  )  # Define hierarchies dims <- list(   REGION = hier_create("Total", nodes = c("North", "South")),   INDUSTRY = hier_create("Total", nodes = c("Tech", "Mfg", "Retail")) )  # Initialize and perturb sdc <- rn_setup(   data = dt,    dim_list = dims,    num_vars = c("turnover", "assets"),    sensitive_params = list(n_threshold = 5) ) #> ℹ Initialization started... #> ℹ Creating structural mapping via sdcTable... #> ℹ Identifying base cells (leaf nodes)... #> ✔ Initialization complete.  sdc$perturb("turnover") #> ℹ Only n_threshold active. #> ℹ Starting rebalancing for 6 base cells... #> ℹ Aggregating results through hierarchy via sdcTable... #> ✔ Perturbation for `turnover` complete. sdc$perturb("assets") #> ℹ Only n_threshold active. #> ℹ Starting rebalancing for 6 base cells... #> ℹ Aggregating results through hierarchy via sdcTable... #> ✔ Perturbation for `assets` complete.

title: Getting Started with rebalancedNoise
text: get_results() method provides two primary ways view data.

title: Getting Started with rebalancedNoise
text: Ideal automated processing visualization ggplot2. uses standardized value columns variable column distinguish turnover assets.
code: res_long <- sdc$get_results(format = "long")  # Standardized names: val_orig, val_pert, diff_final_pct head(res_long[, .(variable, REGION, val_orig, val_pert, is_sens)]) #>    variable REGION  val_orig  val_pert is_sens #>      <char> <char>     <num>     <num>  <lgcl> #> 1: turnover  Total 105576.98 105578.42   FALSE #> 2: turnover  Total  42444.12  42442.35   FALSE #> 3: turnover  Total  32198.80  32199.71   FALSE #> 4: turnover  Total  30934.06  30936.36   FALSE #> 5: turnover  North  52147.99  52148.29   FALSE #> 6: turnover  North  22106.82  22103.80   FALSE

title: Getting Started with rebalancedNoise
text: wide format pivots data hierarchical cell occupies single row. Variable names used prefixes values.

title: Getting Started with rebalancedNoise
text: following variables included results help interpret perturbation quality:

title: Getting Started with rebalancedNoise
text: finalizing tables, use $summarize() method evaluate impact perturbation. provides high-density diagnostic report across three groups: Overall, Non-Sensitive (Rebalanced), Sensitive (Fixed Noise).

title: Getting Started with rebalancedNoise
text: MAPE (Initial vs. Final): Non-Sensitive group, “Initial” MAPE reflects noise rebalancing. “Final” MAPE significantly lower, demonstrating algorithm’s effectiveness. Percentile Comparison: comparing “Initial” “Final” Relative (%) distributions, can visualize “shrinking” effect. successful run show interquartile range (25% 75%) moving towards 0. Tail Risks: Monitor 1% 99% percentiles. identify specific cells hierarchy received disproportionately high noise, can occasionally occur small isolated nodes. Absolute Impact: “Absolute (Units)” distribution translates percentages original scale data, helping assess actual impact table utility.

title: Getting Started with rebalancedNoise
text: EZS engine utilizes C++ OpenMP computationally intensive tasks. number threads (n_threads) resolved rn_setup() remains fixed life object. engine resolves thread count using following priority: Function Argument: rn_setup(..., n_threads = 4) Global Option: options(rn_threads = 4) Environment Variable: Sys.setenv(rn_threads = "4") Automatic Fallback: parallel::detectCores() - 1 (Minimum 1)
code: # Example: Setting a session-wide preference options(rn_threads = 4)  # Initialize - this object is now locked to 4 threads sdc <- rn_setup(dt, dims, "turnover") #> ℹ Initialization started... #> ℹ Creating structural mapping via sdcTable... #> ℹ Identifying base cells (leaf nodes)... #> ✔ Initialization complete.

title: Getting Started with rebalancedNoise
text: Hierarchy Integrity: Aggregations across hierarchy remain additive. Automatic Cleanup: Internal SDC identifiers removed, leaving dimensions results. Calculated Deviations: Percentage deviations rounded 3 decimal places immediate quality checks.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Meindl B (2026). rebalancedNoise: Perturbative SDC Magnitude Tables (EZS Method). R package version 0.1.1, https://sdctools.github.io/rebalancedNoise/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {rebalancedNoise: Perturbative SDC for Magnitude Tables (EZS Method)},   author = {Bernhard Meindl},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.1},   url = {https://sdctools.github.io/rebalancedNoise/}, }

what: Perturbative SDC for Magnitude Tables (EZS Method)
title: Perturbative SDC for Magnitude Tables (EZS Method)
text: rebalancedNoise implements EZS method magnitude tables described Paper Using Perturbative Methods Magnitude Tables Statistical Disclosure Control Sabolová et al. (2025). provides high-performance framework applying record-level noise dynamic rebalancing algorithm preserves data quality non-sensitive cells ensuring additive consistency across complex hierarchies.

title: Perturbative SDC for Magnitude Tables (EZS Method)
text: Hierarchy Integrity: Ensures hierarchical aggregates remain perfectly additive perturbation. Dynamic Rebalancing: Minimizes noise non-sensitive cells “balancing” noise directions within hierarchical nodes. High Performance: Rebalancing algorithm implemented C++ OpenMP support fast processing large inputs. Built-Diagnostics: Powerful $summarize() tools evaluate rebalancing efficiency data utility via high-density statistical reports. Seamless Integration: Designed work directly data.table sdcHierarchies.

title: Perturbative SDC for Magnitude Tables (EZS Method)
Perturbative SDC for Magnitude Tables (EZS Method)
code: library(rebalancedNoise)  # Generate Dummy-Data N <- 100 countries <- c("AT", "DE", "NL", "SE", "FR", "IT") set.seed(1) dt <- data.table(   country = sample(countries, N, replace = TRUE),   turnover = runif(N, 10, 1000),   direction = sample(c(1, -1), N, replace = TRUE),   noise_multiplier = 0.05 )  # Define hierarchy dims <- list(   country = sdcHierarchies::hier_create("Total", nodes = countries) )  # Initialize the Object sdc <- rn_setup(     data = dt,      dim_list = dims,      num_vars = c("turnover"),     sensitive_params = list(n_threshold = 5) )  # Perturb the numerical variable sdc$perturb("turnover")  # Show summarize-statistics (initial vs. final perturbation) sdc$summarize("turnover")  # extract results sdc$get_results("turnover", format = "long") # or "wide"

title: Perturbative SDC for Magnitude Tables (EZS Method)
text: detailed walkthrough method, multi-variable handling, parallelization settings (via options() environment variables), please see full package vignette:

title: rebalancedNoise 0.1.1
text: Initial ordering $rebalance() now based impact (abs(orig * mult))

title: rebalancedNoise 0.1.0
text: Initial release rebalancedNoise package. Implementation EZS method (Sabolova et al., 2025) magnitude tables. Contains dynamic rebalancing algorithm minimize noise non-sensitive cells preserve additive consistency across hierarchies. Method $perturb(): Perturbs numeric variable performs rebalancing Supports Wide Long output formats Standardized column naming convention (e.g., [var]_orig, [var]_pert). Includes metadata variables: is_internal identify internal is_sens track sensitivity status. Computes percentage deviations rebalancing step. Added rn_setup() initialize rebalancedNoise object support multiple numerical variables. Added package vignette get started vignette("getting-started", package = "rebalancedNoise") can configured using argument n_threads rn_setup() options(rn_threads) / Sys.getenv("rn_threads")
